output.final_answer:
Here are the top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes:

| Rank | Model Name | Likes | Downloads | Parameters | License |
|------|-----------|-------|-----------|------------|----------|
| 1 | deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1 | 13.4k | 5.59M | 685B | see model card |
| 2 | meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B | 6.57k | 915k | 8B | see model card |
| 3 | meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct | 6.07k | 9.87M | 8B | see model card |

DeepSeek-R1 leads significantly with 13.4k likes, followed by Meta's Llama-3-8B and Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct models. Note: License details are not displayed on the model listing cards; please visit each model's card for full license information.

prompt_sent:
You are the Formatter skill. You are the conventional TERMINAL node of
every DAG. Your job is to produce the final user-facing answer from
whatever upstream nodes have provided.

You make no tool calls. The user's original query appears under
USER_QUERY. Upstream results appear under INPUTS.

Procedure:
  1. Read USER_QUERY.
  2. Read INPUTS and decide which fields / findings answer the query.
  3. Write the user-facing answer in plain English. Adapt the format
     (numbered list, comparison table, one paragraph) to what the
     question actually asked.

Output schema (JSON, no prose, no markdown fences):

  {
    "final_answer": "<the answer the user sees>"
  }

Rules:
  - This is the LAST node. Do not add successors.
  - The answer must be answerable from INPUTS alone. If an upstream
    node returned `(not found)` or marked itself failed, say so plainly
    to the user rather than inventing.
  - Cite sources only when an upstream node included them (Researcher
    nodes do; Retriever nodes do). Do not invent URLs.
  - If an upstream `verifier` node is present, respect its verdict: when it
    is "holds", you may state the claim confidently; when it is "refuted" or
    "unsupported", add a short honest caveat (e.g. "note: this could not be
    confirmed from the evidence") and reflect its `reason`.

USER_QUERY: Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes

MEMORY HITS (1 from FAISS):
  - [scratchpad] Task to compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models by downloads, reporting name, downloads, likes, license, and parameter count in a table
      source: user_query
      raw: Compare the top 3 text-generation models on Hugging Face sorted by downloads - report name, downloads, likes, license and parameter count in a comparison table

INPUTS:
[
  {
    "id": "USER_QUERY",
    "kind": "query",
    "value": "Compare top 3 Hugging Face text-generation models sorted by likes"
  },
  {
    "id": "n:18",
    "kind": "upstream",
    "skill": "distiller",
    "output": {
      "fields": {
        "model_1_name": "deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1",
        "model_1_url": "https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1",
        "model_1_params": "685B",
        "model_1_downloads": "5.59M",
        "model_1_likes": "13.4k",
        "model_1_license": "see model card",
        "model_2_name": "meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B",
        "model_2_url": "https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B",
        "model_2_params": "8B",
        "model_2_downloads": "915k",
        "model_2_likes": "6.57k",
        "model_2_license": "see model card",
        "model_3_name": "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
        "model_3_url": "https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct",
        "model_3_params": "8B",
        "model_3_downloads": "9.87M",
        "model_3_likes": "6.07k",
        "model_3_license": "see model card"
      },
      "rationale": "Browser node n:17 extracted the top 3 text-generation models sorted by Most Likes from Hugging Face listing cards. Model names, parameter sizes, downloads, and likes counts are parsed directly from the card content. URLs are constructed from the org/model identifiers shown in the browser output. License information is not displayed on listing cards, so all license fields are set to 'see model card' as instructed."
    }
  }
]
